history.text: How do I make carbonara for 3 people?

To make carbonara for three people, you'll need about 400 grams of spaghetti, 120–150 grams of cured pork like guanciale or pancetta, 200 grams of pecorino romano cheese, 100 grams of Parmigiano-Reggiano, three eggs, plenty of black pepper, and fresh parsley for garnish. Start by cutting the pork into small cubes and frying them in a large pan over medium heat until they're crisp and golden, removing them once cooked and setting them aside with the rendered fat. Boil the spaghetti in salted water until it's al dente, then reserve a cup of the starchy cooking water before draining. Whisk the eggs together with both cheeses and a generous amount of black pepper to create a creamy mixture. Combine the hot pasta with the crispy pork and a splash of the reserved pasta water in a warm pan or bowl, then quickly add the egg-cheese mixture off the heat while tossing continuously; the residual warmth will thicken the sauce without scrambling the eggs. Finish with more black pepper and chopped parsley, adjusting seasoning with salt if needed, and serve immediately.
